title_en: Organization by Companion: The Musnad's Structure
content_en:
The organizational principle of the Musnad — arranging hadiths by the Companion who transmitted them rather than by topic — distinguishes it sharply from the Sahih of al-Bukhari, the Sunan of Abu Dawud, and most other major hadith works, which organize their contents by legal or thematic category. This structural choice was deliberate and carries significant implications for how the Musnad is used.

The arrangement by Companion reflects the way early hadith transmission actually worked. A scholar in the first two centuries of Islam would say: 'From Abu Hurairah, from the Prophet, he said...' The Companion was the first link in the chain, the person whose authority grounded the report. By organizing the Musnad around Companions, Ahmad preserved this natural transmission structure. It also means that a researcher who wants to know what Abu Bakr narrated from the Prophet, or what Anas ibn Malik reported, can consult that Companion's section directly — a benefit for biographical and historical research as much as for legal inquiry.

The Musnad opens with what Ahmad and his tradition considered the most honored section: the hadiths of the four rightly guided caliphs — Abu Bakr as-Siddiq, Umar ibn al-Khattab, Uthman ibn Affan, and Ali ibn Abi Talib. This ordering reflects the Sunni theological consensus on the relative merit of the Companions, a topic Ahmad himself addressed in his theological writings. Following the four caliphs, the Musnad presents the hadiths of the ten Companions promised paradise (al-asharah al-mubashsharun bil-jannah) as a group, including Abd ar-Rahman ibn Awf, Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, Sa'id ibn Zayd, Abu Ubaydah ibn al-Jarrah, Talhah ibn Ubaydullah, and az-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam.

After the most distinguished Companions, the Musnad continues with other Companions broadly organized by how early they embraced Islam or how close they were to the Prophet. The Ansar (the Companions from Madinah who received the emigrants) have their own sections. Women Companions — Aisha, Umm Salamah, Hafsa, and many others — are included, usually toward the latter portions, though their sections contain hadiths of enormous importance, particularly Aisha's extensive transmissions on prayer, purity, and domestic life with the Prophet.

Practically speaking, the Companion-by-Companion organization makes the Musnad somewhat difficult to use for legal research without indices. A scholar seeking hadiths on a specific topic — say, the prayer at night or the rules of zakah — must know which Companions are most likely to have narrated on those subjects, then search their respective sections. This limitation was recognized early, and scholars produced topical indexes and cross-references to the Musnad for this purpose. The most important of these is the Ithaf al-Maharah by Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani, which maps the Musnad's hadiths to their topics and cross-references them with other collections.

Despite its organizational challenges, the Companion-based structure gives the Musnad a quality that topically arranged works lack: it allows the reader to encounter the Prophet's teachings as specific Companions received and remembered them, preserving something of the personal and historical texture of early Islamic transmission.
content_id:
Prinsip pengorganisasian Musnad — menyusun hadis berdasarkan sahabat yang meriwayatkannya, bukan berdasarkan topik — membedakannya secara tajam dari Sahih Al-Bukhari, Sunan Abu Dawud, dan sebagian besar kitab hadis besar lainnya yang mengorganisasikan isinya berdasarkan kategori hukum atau tematik. Pilihan struktural ini dilakukan secara sengaja dan membawa implikasi penting bagi cara Musnad digunakan.

Susunan berdasarkan sahabat mencerminkan cara periwayatan hadis pada masa-masa awal Islam sesungguhnya berlangsung. Seorang ulama pada dua abad pertama Islam akan berkata: 'Dari Abu Hurairah, dari Nabi ﷺ, beliau bersabda...' Sahabat adalah mata rantai pertama dalam sanad, orang yang otoritasnya mendasari laporan tersebut. Dengan mengorganisasikan Musnad di seputar sahabat, Imam Ahmad memelihara struktur periwayatan yang alami ini. Ini juga berarti bahwa seorang peneliti yang ingin mengetahui apa yang diriwayatkan Abu Bakar al-Siddiq dari Nabi ﷺ, atau apa yang dilaporkan Anas ibn Malik, dapat langsung merujuk bagian sahabat tersebut — suatu manfaat bagi penelitian biografi dan sejarah, tidak kurang dari manfaatnya bagi penelitian hukum.

Musnad dibuka dengan bagian yang oleh Imam Ahmad dan tradisinya dianggap paling mulia: hadis-hadis keempat khalifah yang mendapat petunjuk — Abu Bakar al-Siddiq (ra), Umar ibn al-Khattab (ra), Utsman ibn Affan (ra), dan Ali ibn Abi Thalib (ra). Urutan ini mencerminkan konsensus teologis ahlus sunnah wal jamaah mengenai keutamaan relatif para sahabat, sebuah topik yang juga dibahas oleh Imam Ahmad sendiri dalam tulisan-tulisan teologinya. Setelah keempat khalifah, Musnad menyajikan hadis-hadis sepuluh sahabat yang dijanjikan surga (al-asharah al-mubasysyarun bil-jannah) secara berkelompok, termasuk Abd ar-Rahman ibn Awf, Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, Sa'id ibn Zayd, Abu Ubaidah ibn al-Jarrah, Thalhah ibn Ubaidullah, dan az-Zubair ibn al-Awwam.

Setelah sahabat-sahabat paling utama, Musnad melanjutkan dengan sahabat-sahabat lain yang secara umum diurutkan berdasarkan seberapa awal mereka memeluk Islam atau seberapa dekat mereka dengan Nabi ﷺ. Kaum Anshar — sahabat dari Madinah yang menyambut para muhajirin — memiliki bagian-bagian tersendiri. Sahabat perempuan — Aisyah, Ummu Salamah, Hafshah, dan banyak lainnya — turut dicantumkan, biasanya di bagian-bagian akhir, meski bagian-bagian mereka memuat hadis-hadis yang sangat penting, terutama riwayat-riwayat Aisyah yang sangat luas mengenai salat, bersuci, dan kehidupan rumah tangga bersama Nabi ﷺ.

Secara praktis, susunan per-sahabat membuat Musnad agak sulit digunakan untuk penelitian hukum tanpa indeks. Seorang ulama yang mencari hadis tentang topik tertentu — misalnya salat malam atau aturan-aturan zakat — harus mengetahui sahabat mana yang paling mungkin meriwayatkan tentang subjek-subjek tersebut, lalu menelusuri bagian-bagian mereka masing-masing. Keterbatasan ini diakui sejak dini, dan para ulama pun menyusun indeks tematik serta referensi silang untuk Musnad. Yang paling penting di antaranya adalah Ithaf al-Maharah karya Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani, yang memetakan hadis-hadis Musnad ke dalam topik-topik dan merujuk silang dengan koleksi-koleksi lain.

Terlepas dari tantangan pengorganisasiannya, struktur berbasis sahabat memberikan Musnad suatu kualitas yang tidak dimiliki karya-karya bertematik: ia memungkinkan pembaca untuk menjumpai ajaran-ajaran Nabi ﷺ sebagaimana diterima dan diingat oleh sahabat-sahabat tertentu, menjaga sesuatu dari tekstur personal dan historis periwayatan Islam awal.
